title: 1  Getting Started with remote sensing
crumbs: 1  Getting Started with remote sensing

title: 1  Getting Started with remote sensing
text:
Summary



Briefly summarise the key ideas from this week’s lecture or material.
Include: - Main concepts or methods introduced - Important datasets, tools, or techniques - Questions that arose while learning - Potential limitations or benefits of the approaches discussed
Remember: this should not simply restate the lecture but demonstrate understanding and critical thinking.








Applications



Discuss how the concepts, data, or methods have been used elsewhere.
Possible angles: - Examples from academic literature - Applications in policy or government - Real-world case studies - How other studies have implemented similar approaches
Include references where relevant.








Reflection



Your personal reflection on the material.
Consider: - What did you learn this week? - What did you find interesting or surprising? - What aspects were difficult or unclear? - How might this knowledge influence your own work or research? - What would you like to explore further?
